pi_items.source: VitalDB official database (vitaldb.net) - Case #3 confirmed to exist
VitalDB Case #3 actual recording duration measured via VitalFile.dtstart/dtend
VitalDB Case #3 analyzed: Primus/ETCO2 first valid >0 at 235.7s, Solar8000/ETCO2 first valid at 487.7s. Both within claimed window.
VitalDB Case #3 analyzed: Primus/ETCO2 last valid >0 at 4128.8s, Solar8000/ETCO2 last at 4139.8s. Both are OUTSIDE the claimed [7000, 12000] window.
Cannot verify without running the task - output file is created during task execution

Cannot verify without running the task - event markers are user-created annotations

privileged_info_summary: VitalDB Case #3 is a real surgical case from Seoul National University Hospital's public VitalDB database, with a total duration of 73.24 minutes. The EtCO2 waveform (both Primus and Solar8000 monitors) shows valid ventilation signals within the claimed start window (235.7-487.7 seconds vs. claimed 100-1200 seconds), but the end window claim is CONTRADICTED: the last valid EtCO2 signals occur at 4128.8 seconds (Primus) and 4139.8 seconds (Solar8000), which are approximately 3 hours into the recording, NOT in the claimed 7000-12000 second (1.9-3.3 hour) window. The claim appears to be off by roughly 2870 seconds (48 minutes).
